what_it_does: It is a design-draft document that defines 'part' as a unit of cognition and proposes a staged multi-part voice response so that brain generation and TTS/playback can overlap within the existing blocking model-call fabric, reducing time-to-first-audio without token-level streaming.
observations: Today's /api/voice/stream waits for the full brain reply before any TTS runs because fabric/transport.py sends stream:false and Suite.chat returns a complete reply.
The design introduces a 'part' as a complete sub-generation (a finished string, not a token) and uses each completed part as the TTS streaming unit.
It proposes a single HTTP request with a brain-producer thread enqueueing parts and the handler thread consuming them as the sole ordered ndjson emitter.
Chunk idx must stay monotonic across all parts so the frontend's playCursorRef in-order scheduling remains unchanged.
Trial recording must still happen once per turn using the assembled full reply, not per part.
Suite.chat_parts must append exactly one assistant turn and emit one chat event, avoiding N conversation turns for one spoken turn.
Cancellation via client_gone() is extended to gate the next part generation as well as the next synth.
Token-level brain streaming and streaming STT are explicitly declared out of scope.
standouts: The 'part' abstraction lets brain↔TTS overlap without changing the model transport from stream:false.
A prebuffer knob (COMPANY_VOICE_PREBUFFER_PARTS) explicitly trades time-to-first-audio against gapless-playback risk.
Strong invariant: one logical turn / one chat record / one trial record even when the brain runs N internal sub-generations.
Heavy use of file:line citations to anchor the design in the current implementation.
conventions: YAML front matter with type, module, aliases, tags, status, relates-to.
Numbered sections and subsections with descriptive headings.
File:line evidence cited for every claim about current behavior.
ASCII latency diagrams and algebraic cost tables.
Explicit 'out of scope' and 'preserved invariants' lists.
concerns: Gap risk: if a later part generates slower than earlier audio plays, the listener hears silence.
Threading complexity from producer/consumer queue plus cancellation across a blocking Suite.chat call.
Risk of chat/event-log pollution if chat_parts accidentally records each part as a separate turn.
Design is unbuilt (status design-draft) and depends on a new Suite method not yet specified.
notes: This file is a buildable design draft that pinpoints the exact blocking point in the voice pipeline and proposes overlapping brain and TTS by staging complete 'parts' rather than streaming tokens.
questions: What is the concrete prompt/schema that turns a single user turn into a sequence of bounded parts?
What default and bounds should COMPANY_VOICE_PREBUFFER_PARTS have?
How does cancellation abort an in-flight Suite.chat part generation, or does it only stop between parts?
How are tool calls grounded across multiple parts without creating multiple turns?
What delimiter/joiner is used when assembling parts into the final recorded reply?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it is presented as a design-draft for staged multi-part voice coupling and delivers current-state analysis plus a specified, additive change set.
apparent_themes: Latency reduction through concurrency while keeping existing contracts intact.
Part-level cognition as a pragmatic step toward token-level streaming.
Record-once discipline for trial transcripts, chat history, and SSE chat events.
Evidence-grounded design anchored in file:line traces.
Explicit boundary-setting to avoid scope creep.
intention_signals: It is trying to make spoken replies feel faster by letting the brain and TTS run concurrently, using a 'part' abstraction that fits the existing blocking model-call fabric while protecting transcript and audio ordering invariants.
novelty: The idea of using complete sub-generations ('parts') as the streaming unit to overlap brain and TTS without requiring token-level streaming or transport changes, plus a prebuffer knob that exposes the TTFA-vs-gap-risk tradeoff.
contribution: A concrete, evidence-backed staging design that defines the 'part' unit, specifies a single-emitter producer/consumer integration, preserves in-order playback, iOS unlock, and single-turn recording, and models the latency/gap-risk tradeoff with a tunable prebuffer.
summary_for_embedding: Design draft for Voice Stream Coupling that defines 'parts' as complete sub-generations to overlap brain generation with TTS streaming inside the existing /api/voice/stream ndjson contract, preserving in-order playback, single-turn recording, and iOS audio-context unlock while leaving token-level streaming out of scope.
intention_for_embedding: To specify a buildable intermediate latency improvement for spoken replies by staging multi-part cognition within one HTTP voice stream without changing the model transport.
